content.problemStatement:
Stack has a positive integer P(P>1)P(P > 1)P(P>1).

He starts a game where his initial score is 000.

In the ithi^{th}ith turn, with a probability of 12\frac{1}{2}21​, Stack adds 1Pi−1\frac{1}{P^{i-1}}Pi−11​ to his score.

Stack stops after NNN turns.

Let SiS_iSi​ be his score after iii turns and F(i)F(i)F(i) denote the expected value of Si2S_i^2Si2​.

For each integer i(1≤i≤N)i(1 \leq i \leq N)i(1≤i≤N), find the value of F(i)F(i)F(i) modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format
The only line of input contains two space-separated positive integers NNN and PPP, as mentioned in the statement.



Output Format
Print NNN space-separated integers. For each integer i(1≤i≤N)i(1 \leq i \leq N)i(1≤i≤N), output the value of F(i)F(i)F(i) modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Formally, let M=998244353M=998244353M=998244353. It can be shown that the answer can be expressed as an irreducible fraction xy\frac{x}{y}yx​, where xxx and yyy are integers and y≢0(modM)y \not \equiv 0 \pmod{M}y≡0(modM).

Output the integer equal to x⋅y−1 mod Mx \cdot y^{-1} \bmod M x⋅y−1modM.

In other words, output such an integer zzz that 0≤z<M0 \leq z < M0≤z<M and z⋅y≡x(modM)z \cdot y \equiv x \pmod{M}z⋅y≡x(modM).
.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
S1S_1S1​ can be 000 or 111 with equal probability.
Thus F(1)=0⋅02+1⋅12=12F(1)=\frac{0 \cdot 0}{2}+\frac{1 \cdot 1}{2} = \frac{1}{2}F(1)=20⋅0​+21⋅1​=21​.
F(2)=78F(2)=\frac{7}{8}F(2)=87​.
